name: Textron Inc.
executive_summary: Textron is a diversified aerospace, defence, and industrial conglomerate whose quantitative profile fits a value/GARP buyer: P/E 17.2, P/B 2.0, debt/equity 53%, and ROE 12.3%, with seven classic value models passing. Revenue reached $14.8 billion in FY2025 (year ended 3 January 2026), up from $13.7 billion in FY2024, supported by a record $19.2 billion backlog and accelerating Bell MV-75 military development. The valuation hook is a cyclically depressed multi-industry portfolio—aviation recovery, defence rearmament, and a planned Industrial separation—trading below many pure-play aerospace peers. The central debate is whether defence-led growth and portfolio simplification will unlock value faster than MV-75 programme accounting, government funding risk, tariff exposure, and Kautex/auto cyclicality erode margins.
investment_thesis:
For a value investor, TXT offers a rare combination of cheapness, balance-sheet quality, and identifiable catalysts. The screen passes five factor families—cheapness, quality, dividend, GARP, and risk—with Graham Enterprising, Neff PEGY, Quality Value, Dividend Growth, Dreman Contrarian, Composite Value, and Financial Health all triggered. At P/E 17.2 and P/B 1.96, the market prices Textron as a mid-quality industrial rather than a defence-aerospace compounder with $19 billion of contracted future revenue.

Business quality is underwritten by segment leadership: Cessna/Beechcraft in general aviation, Bell in military/commercial rotorcraft (including the Army MV-75/FLRAA programme), Textron Systems in unmanned systems and land vehicles, and Kautex in automotive fuel systems. FY2025 revenue growth of roughly 8% (filings) coincided with earnings acceleration—diluted EPS rose from $4.33 (FY2024, Yahoo fallback) to $5.11 (FY2025, Yahoo fallback)—while free cash flow recovered to $929 million (Yahoo fallback) from $650 million in FY2024. Aggressive buybacks ($822 million in FY2025 per Yahoo) and minimal cash dividends ($0.08/share annually per Q1 2026 10-Q) signal a capital-return model aligned with value screens; note the screen’s 9.0% “Yield” metric appears to reflect total shareholder yield rather than cash dividend yield.

The buy case strengthens on interim trends: Q1 FY2026 revenue rose 12% year-on-year to $3.70 billion (Q1 2026 10-Q), with Textron Aviation up sharply on jet deliveries and Bell up on MV-75, partially offset by Industrial. Management announced on 30 April 2026 a planned separation of the Industrial segment within 12–18 months, which could unlock sum-of-the-parts value by isolating lower-multiple Kautex and specialized vehicles from higher-quality aviation/defence assets.
financial_review:
**Annual results (10-K)**

Primary filing bodies are available for FY2025 (filed 11 February 2026) and FY2024 (filed 6 February 2025), but both 10-K extracts are truncated before Item 7 MD&A and Item 8 consolidated financial statements. Business-section disclosures are used where available; detailed annual P&L, balance sheet, and cash-flow line items below are sourced from `financials_annual.json` (Yahoo) with explicit fallback noted.

| Metric | FY2025 (Jan 2026) | FY2024 (Dec 2024) | Source |
|--------|-------------------|-------------------|--------|
| Revenue | $14.8bn | $13.7bn | FY2025/FY2024 10-K business sections; Yahoo confirms $14.80bn / $13.70bn |
| Operating income | $1,001m | $855m | Yahoo fallback |
| Net income | $921m | $824m | Yahoo fallback |
| Diluted EPS | $5.11 | $4.33 | Yahoo fallback |
| Free cash flow | $929m | $650m | Yahoo fallback |
| Total assets | $18.13bn | $16.84bn | Yahoo fallback |
| Cash & equivalents | $2.03bn | $1.44bn | Yahoo fallback |
| Total debt | $3.88bn | $3.59bn | Yahoo fallback |
| Net debt | $1.85bn | $2.15bn | Yahoo fallback |

FY2025 revenue growth of ~8% was broad-based. The FY2025 10-K states total revenues were $14.8 billion. Backlog at end-FY2024 (FY2024 10-K, primary) totalled $17.9 billion: Textron Aviation $7.8bn, Bell $7.5bn, Textron Systems $2.6bn—up 29% year-on-year, driven by Bell military programmes. FY2025 year-end backlog by segment is not present in the truncated FY2025 10-K body.

Segment realignment: effective 4 January 2026, Textron eAviation was absorbed into other operating segments (FY2025 10-K). Powersports (Arctic Cat) was sold in April 2025 (Q1 FY2025 10-Q).

**Interim results (10-Q)**

Six quarterly filing bodies are available (Q2–Q3 FY2024 through Q1 FY2026). Key trends:

*Q3 FY2025 (nine months ended 27 September 2025):* Revenue $10.62bn (+5% vs $10.09bn prior year); net income $686m; diluted EPS from continuing operations $3.79 vs $3.56. Backlog (remaining performance obligations) $19.1bn. Balance sheet at 27 September 2025: total assets $17.38bn; manufacturing cash $1.45bn; manufacturing long-term debt $3.04bn.

*Q1 FY2026 (three months ended 4 April 2026):* Revenue $3.70bn (+12% vs $3.31bn); net income $220m (+6%); diluted EPS $1.25 vs $1.13. Segment revenue: Textron Aviation $1.49bn (+22%), Bell $1.07bn (+9%), Textron Systems $338m (+13%), Industrial $786m (flat, reflecting Powersports exit). Segment profit: Aviation $154m, Bell $72m, Systems $42m, Industrial $40m; total segment profit $320m vs $256m prior year. Backlog $19.2bn (77% expected through 2027). U.S. Government revenue was $1.16bn (31% of Q1 revenue), up from $924m (28%) in Q1 FY2025.

**Balance sheet and cash flow (filings)**

At 4 April 2026 (Q1 2026 10-Q): total assets $18.14bn; total liabilities $10.14bn; manufacturing long-term debt $3.11bn plus current portion $355m; consolidated cash $1.61bn. Q1 is seasonally cash-consuming: net cash used in operating activities was $117m (working capital build, including $289m inventory increase), versus $124m used in Q1 FY2025. Share repurchases were $168m in Q1 FY2026; dividends $3m ($0.02/share).

Interest expense is rising modestly ($34m in Q1 FY2026 vs $29m prior year) but remains manageable relative to earnings. Non-service pension income ($70m in Q1 FY2026) continues as a P&L tailwind.

**Data gaps:** FY2025 10-K consolidated statements and full MD&A are absent from filing body extracts. No going-concern language was found in available bodies. Eight 8-K filings in the index have no downloadable bodies.
risks_and_flags:
**Programme and contract risk.** The Q1 FY2026 10-Q flags an expected unfavourable MV-75 cumulative catch-up adjustment of $60–110 million upon award of the largely fixed-price Low-Rate Initial Production option (anticipated late 2026/early 2027), though management states the overall programme remains profit-positive. Separately, Bell received U.S. Army notice on 28 April 2026 that an additional $350 million in MV-75 funding is being requested; if unavailable, Bell could face a stop-work order, with funds expected to exhaust in Q3 FY2026 before the U.S. government fiscal year-end.

**Government concentration.** U.S. Government contracts generated ~25% of FY2024 consolidated revenue (FY2024 10-K). Debarment, audit findings, or budget delays could affect Bell and Textron Systems disproportionately.

**Cyclical and end-market exposure.** Textron Aviation depends on business-jet demand; Kautex on global auto production and EV transition; Industrial on golf/utility vehicles. Q1 FY2026 gross margin fell 100bp on inflation and higher LIFO charges despite revenue growth.

**Trade policy.** Management notes U.S. tariff changes from 2025 onward; 69% of FY2025 revenue was U.S.-generated. To date, tariff impact has not been material, but supply-chain and pricing risk persists. A February 2026 Supreme Court ruling on IEEPA tariffs is being monitored.

**Portfolio execution.** The 30 April 2026 Industrial separation announcement carries no assurance on timing, structure, or completion. Failure to execute could leave a lower-multiple drag on the group multiple.

**Legal and environmental.** Commitments and contingencies language (Q1 FY2026 10-Q) states existing proceedings—including environmental matters—are not expected to have a material effect on financial position; government contractor audits could still result in suspension or debarment in severe cases.

**Pension.** Defined-benefit plans remain a long-term obligation, though current non-service pension income benefits reported earnings.

**Screen-specific caveat.** The 9.0% screen “Yield” should not be read as a cash dividend yield; actual dividends are nominal, with capital return dominated by buybacks.
news_highlights:
Coverage over the past year is moderate, tilted toward strategy, index changes, and sector comparisons rather than deep company-specific flow.

**Strategy and portfolio**
- *Freedom Broker raises Textron stock price target on industrial separation plan* (4 May 2026, Google News/Investing.com) — aligns with the 30 April 2026 Industrial separation intent disclosed in the Q1 10-Q.
- *Textron (TXT) Earnings Growth Outpaces Revenue And Tests Bullish Margin Narratives* (1 May 2026, simplywall.st).
- *What Textron (TXT)'s Revenue Miss and EBITDA Beat Means For Shareholders* (11 December 2025, Yahoo Finance).

**Operations and contracts**
- *Textron (TXT) Launches TUG MX4 Electric Baggage Tractor* (15 July 2026, GuruFocus).
- *Bell signs agreements for three Bell 429 helicopters in U.K., Estonia* (22 April 2026, Seeking Alpha).
- *Is Textron Expanding Its Presence in the Helicopter Market?* (1 July 2026, Yahoo Finance) — Bell 407GXi delivery to Texas DPS.
- *Bell Announces New UK and Ireland Sales Representation* (31 October 2025, Textron IR).

**Market positioning and sentiment**
- *Does Textron's (TXT) Index Exit Quietly Recast Its Aviation and Defense Investment Profile?* (2 July 2026, Yahoo Finance) and *How Investors Are Reacting To Textron (TXT) Being Removed From The Russell 1000 Dynamic Index* (9 July 2026, simplywall.st) — index removal may affect passive flows.
- *Hexcel vs. Textron: Which Key Industrial Supplier Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?* (13 July 2026, Yahoo Finance).
- *Textron - At A Somewhat Lower Valuation, I'd Be In (NYSE:TXT)* (4 July 2026, Seeking Alpha).
- *2 Unpopular Stocks That Deserve a Second Chance and 1 We Find Risky* (9 July 2026, Yahoo Finance) — TXT mentioned among unpopular names.
- *AEGON ASSET MANAGEMENT UK Plc Buys Shares of 391,386 Textron Inc.* (21 June 2026, MarketBeat) — new institutional stake.

**Macro/sector context (indirect)**
- Defence spending and drone-related headlines (Howmet, AeroVironment, BAE) provide sector tailwinds but are not TXT-specific.

No material management turnover or major M&A beyond the Powersports divestiture and announced Industrial separation review was identified in the manifest. Company-specific news is adequate but not deep; several items are syndicated commentary or technical trading pieces.
research_rationale: Filings confirm accelerating revenue, record backlog, manageable leverage, and a credible Industrial separation catalyst that support the quantitative buy signal, partially offset by MV-75 funding/accounting risk, cyclical Industrial exposure, and incomplete annual filing extracts requiring Yahoo fallback for full-year statements.
